tool.name: hackerone_intel
tool.description:
Consult the local HackerOne intelligence engine — 12,340 disclosed HackerOne reports distilled into ranked hunt plans, per-bug-class playbooks, per-technology and per-feature intel, cross-report bug-chain intelligence, and real report citations.
ROOT AGENT ONLY. Use this as a consult-first step BEFORE proposing a methodology or spawning sub-agents on a target, and to ground hypotheses in disclosed-report priors. Sub-agents cannot use this tool.
Typical flow: start with mode="plan" (pass tech / feature / target) for a ranked, evidence-backed hypothesis list, then drill in with mode="playbook" / "profile" / "feature" for the how-to, and mode="class" / "search" / "show" for the real reports behind it.
READ-ONLY. Returns a dict: {"success": true, "mode": ..., "output": "", "truncated"?: true} on success, or {"success": false, "error": "..."} on failure (including when the KB is not configured on this host — it degrades gracefully and never crashes).
Modes and the params each uses: - plan — needs at least one of tech / feature; target labels the plan header; limit caps hypotheses. Returns ranked hypotheses + chains + suggested order. - playbook — query = bug-class slug (e.g. "ssrf"). Hunting playbook for that class. - profile — query = technology slug (e.g. "nextjs", "aws"). Tech intelligence profile. - feature — query = feature slug (e.g. "billing", "rbac"). Feature intelligence profile. - chains — no query. Cross-report bug-chain intelligence. - recon — no query. Recon-value ranking. - search — query = free text; limit. Full-text search across reports. - class — query = bug-class slug; limit. Disclosed reports in that class. - show — report_id = numeric report id. Full writeup + metadata (can be long; raise max_chars to read it all). - tech — query = bug-class slug. Normalized techniques extracted for that class. - assets — query = host or technology term. Reports touching that asset/tech. - analysis — query = analysis name (optional; defaults to a cross-report synthesis). - classes — no query. Bug-class distribution across the corpus. - programs — limit. Most-disclosed programs.
Output is truncated at max_chars (default 16000) with "truncated": true set; raise max_chars only when you deliberately want a full long result (e.g. a specific "show").
Known limitation: a query that is EXACTLY one of the engine's flag strings (--class, --program, --severity, --limit, --bounty, --kind) is misparsed by the underlying script and returns wrong/empty results. Avoid passing a bare flag string as query.

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: Which KB subcommand to run: one of plan, playbook, profile, feature, chains, recon, search, class, show, tech, assets, analysis, classes, programs. (The write-path "outcome" subcommand is intentionally not available.)

parameter.name: query
parameter.description: Free-text term or slug, per mode: bug-class slug for playbook/class/tech, technology slug for profile, feature slug for feature, free text for search, host/tech for assets, analysis name for analysis. Leave empty for plan/show/chains/recon/classes/programs.

parameter.name: report_id
parameter.description: Numeric HackerOne report id. Required for mode="show".

parameter.name: tech
parameter.description: Comma-separated technology slugs/names for mode="plan" (e.g. "nextjs,aws"). At least one of tech or feature is required for plan.

parameter.name: feature
parameter.description: Comma-separated feature slugs for mode="plan" (e.g. "billing,rbac"). At least one of tech or feature is required for plan.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: Target label used in the plan header, for mode="plan" (e.g. a domain). Optional.

parameter.name: limit
parameter.description: Result/hypothesis cap for search / class / programs / plan. Default 20.

parameter.name: max_chars
parameter.description: Max characters of engine output to return before truncation. Default 16000. Raise it when you deliberately want a full, long result.
